page: Google Cloud Platform m4n-megamem-56 is a memory-optimized virtual machine belonging to the m4n server family. It is built on the x86_64 CPU architecture and features 56 dedicated virtual CPUs. The instance is equipped with 744.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high resource density of approximately 13.29 GB of RAM per vCPU core.

This server configuration does not include local storage or integrated GPU hardware accelerators, meaning all storage requirements must be met via network-attached block storage options. Additionally, the instance does not come with complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Given the high memory-to-vCPU ratio, the m4n-megamem-56 is engineered for memory-intensive enterprise workloads. It is suitable for large-scale in-memory databases, real-time data analytics, and memory-bound enterprise resource planning applications. The dedicated CPU allocation ensures predictable compute performance, making it a qualitatively cost-efficient choice for workloads requiring massive RAM footprints without the overhead of excessive CPU cores.
description: Google Cloud Platform m4n-megamem-56 is a memory-optimized virtual machine from the m4n family, designed for resource-intensive enterprise applications. Operating on the x86_64 architecture, it features 56 dedicated vCPUs and 744.0 GB of RAM, providing a high memory-to-core ratio of 13.29 GB per vCPU. The instance does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, relying entirely on network-attached storage solutions. With its dedicated CPU allocation, this server delivers predictable performance for memory-bound workloads. It is particularly suited for hosting large in-memory databases, complex data analytics pipelines, and enterprise resource planning systems that require substantial memory capacity relative to compute resources.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform m4n-megamem-56 is a memory-optimized server featuring 56 dedicated vCPUs, 744 GB of RAM, and an x86_64 architecture, designed for large-scale in-memory databases.

tagline: A memory-optimized virtual machine featuring a high RAM-to-vCPU ratio for demanding in-memory databases and enterprise applications.
